content_of_post_to_answer: A person can lose years waiting for the perfect moment to start. Is overthinking more dangerous than failure itself?
answer: Overthinking often feels like preparation, but its hidden cost is that it lets us experience the discomfort of failure without ever gaining the information that action provides. A failed attempt teaches reality; endless analysis only teaches our fears. I’ve explored this pattern in more detail on my profile for those who want to examine why intelligent people sometimes get trapped in thinking. The perfect moment is usually not found before starting; it is created by starting.
